title: Preview: AFC Wimbledon vs Stockport County - prediction, team news, lineups
text: Stockport County travel to AFC Wimbledon on Wednesday evening in League One Gameweek 16, with the visitors seeking to strengthen their automatic promotion push from fifth place. The hosts have slipped to 20th following recent defeats. Match Context — Stockport suffered disappointment in Sunday's EFL Trophy final, losing 3-1 to Luton Town at Wembley despite taking an early lead. Manager Dave Challinor's side now refocus on league competition, where they maintain a four-point cushion over sixth place and sit four points behind third. However, they haven't won away in the league since January 27 at Blackpool. AFC Wimbledon are under considerable pressure, sitting just three points above the relegation zone after losing six of their last seven league matches. Manager Johnnie Jackson's team has failed to score in four consecutive defeats, most recently losing 1-0 at Burton Albion. Team News — AFC Wimbledon face significant injury concerns with Ryan Johnson, Patrick Bauer, Joe Lewis, Miles Hippolyte, and Omar Bugiel sidelined. Striker Matty Stevens is unavailable for the season due to hamstring injury. Marcus Browne requires assessment regarding minutes management. Stockport are without suspended forward Louie Barry. Defender Josh Dacres-Cogley should return after missing the EFL Trophy final through cup-tie restrictions. Predicted Lineups — AFC Wimbledon: Bishop; Ogundere, Hutchinson, Seddon; Asiimwe, Smith, Reeves, Nelson, Sasu; Maycock, Browne. Stockport County: Addai; Dacres-Cogley, Olowu, Pye, Osborn; Bate, Norwood; Diamond, Mothersille; Wootton, Sidibeh. Prediction: AFC Wimbledon 1-2 Stockport County.
